---
# EXECUTION-SPEC — PRD-093 Windows Production Certification (Gate E2)
# Generated by build-pipeline (lead-architect scaffold + inherited-seam consultation)
# Revision 2 — patched per EXEC-093 audit (P1-1..P1-5, P2-1..P2-5, P3-1)

prd: PRD-093
prd_title: "POS Loyalty Instrument Printing — Windows Production Certification (Gate E2)"
service: LoyaltyService.InstrumentPrinting
mvp_phase: 3

# Intake Authority Chain (PRD-only mode — no FIB-S structured artifact exists)
fib_h: docs/00-vision/epson/prd-093/FIB-H-POS-LOYALTY-PRINTING-WINDOWS-001.md
handoff: docs/00-vision/epson/PRD-WINDOWS-CERTIFICATION-HANDOFF.md

# Precondition (audit P1-1/P1-5) — Linux Phase-1 real-device gate disposition.
# Disposition: retrospective manual-evidence reconciliation (governance authority attestation).
gate_hw_2_precondition:
  status: closed
  disposition: manually_proven_governance_reconciled
  evidence_type: retrospective_manual_acceptance
  rationale: >
    The PRD-092 Linux/CUPS real-device acceptance was performed manually. The gate remained open
    because its governance state was not reconciled (the PRD-092 Implementation Precis carried the
    stale "OPEN" status). This record closes the stale gate; it does NOT claim Windows certification.
  effect: >
    Establishes the known-good Phase-1 physical-printer baseline. Windows Gate E2 remains
    independently mandatory.
  evidence:
    - docs/00-vision/epson/prd-093/GATE-HW-2-RECONCILIATION.md       # retrospective reconciliation artifact
    - docs/10-prd/PRD-093-loyalty-printing-windows-certification.md  # §7.1, §7.3 D5 (governance closure)
    - docs/00-vision/epson/PRD-092-IMPLEMENTATION-PRECIS.md          # stale "OPEN" record being reconciled

# Workstream Definitions
workstreams:
  GATE_0:
    name: Windows Platform Decision Gate (DEC-WIN-01..04)
    description: >
      EXEC design-resolution gate (audit P1-1). Resolves the four technical decisions PRD-093 §7.3
      delegated to EXEC, recording rationale, dependencies, security posture, and packaging
      consequences in a decision ledger. lead-architect owns; consults backend-service-builder,
      rls-expert (security review), devops-pt2. DEC-WIN-01 RAW Winspool binding + native dependency;
      DEC-WIN-02 Windows Service hosting mechanism (SELECT ONE) + least-privilege identity + queue
      permissions; DEC-WIN-03 localhost authentication envelope (caller identity, credential
      provisioning/storage, signed fields, nonce/request-id scope, timestamp tolerance, replay-cache
      lifetime/bound, rotation/revocation, protocol-version binding, pre-spool rejection mapping,
      browser-bundle exclusion); DEC-WIN-04 packaging/signing/update verification/rollback model.
      INVARIANT: Gate 0 may instantiate the PRD contract but may NOT alter lifecycle vocabulary,
      audit schema, printer family, browser fallback, or network scope (FIB-H §L).
    executor: lead-architect
    executor_type: skill
    traces_to: [PRD-093-§7.3-DEC-1, PRD-093-§7.3-DEC-2, PRD-093-§7.3-DEC-3, PRD-093-§7.3-DEC-4]
    depends_on: []
    outputs:
      - docs/00-vision/epson/prd-093/PRD-093-WINDOWS-CERTIFICATION-DECISION-LEDGER.md
    gate: decision-approved
    estimated_complexity: medium

  WS_W1:
    name: windows_spooler adapter (OS-neutral port realization)
    description: >
      New `createWindowsAdapter` behind the unchanged LoyaltyInstrumentPrinter<ReceiptDocument>
      contract. MUST reuse the exported mapAgentResponseToOutcome / mapTransportErrorToOutcome /
      deriveJobKey from cups-adapter.ts — no forked mapper (GATE-PLATFORM-2). Reuses the Phase-1
      renderer and ReceiptDocument unchanged (INV-1). Exports only the OS-neutral port; no
      Windows/winspool types leak above the boundary (GATE-PLATFORM-1).
    executor: backend-service-builder
    executor_type: skill
    traces_to: [FR-1, FR-3, FR-4, NFR-1, NFR-2]
    depends_on: [GATE_0]
    outputs:
      - services/loyalty/printing/adapters/windows-adapter.ts
      - services/loyalty/printing/adapters/index.ts
    gate: type-check
    estimated_complexity: medium

  WS_W2:
    name: Windows RAW print path — WindowsSpooler seam + simulated + CONCRETE native module
    description: >
      A WindowsSpooler realization of the SAME injectable seam as CupsSpooler (agent layer), per the
      DEC-WIN-01 binding. THREE deliverables (audit P1-2): (a) the seam/port; (b) a deterministic
      simulated impl for CI; (c) a CONCRETE native impl module that is delivered code, not invented
      at certification time. The native module opens the configured printer, StartDocPrinter uses
      pDatatype="RAW", StartPagePrinter where the binding requires, WritePrinter writes the COMPLETE
      byte buffer (partial writes rejected or completed deterministically), and
      EndPagePrinter/EndDocPrinter/ClosePrinter cleanup is guaranteed. Byte payload: ESC @ init ->
      rendered text -> feed -> GS V cut, mirroring createEscPosCommandSpooler (FR-2; NOT Out-Printer,
      NOT a generic/text driver). Win32/native diagnostics stay agent-local (INV-4, GATE-PLATFORM-1).
      Gate W-A is MANUAL EXECUTION of this delivered native module on a Windows host — not manual
      creation of it.
      OUTPUT CONTRACT (audit P2-1): the three TypeScript files below are REQUIRED; DEC-WIN-01
      additionally determines the COMPLETE native artifact set (addon source/build files, helper
      executable project files, runtime loader/package declarations, platform-specific build config)
      under services/loyalty/printing/agent/native/. The TypeScript facade alone does NOT satisfy
      WS_W2 — the decision-dependent native artifacts must accompany it.
    executor: backend-service-builder
    executor_type: skill
    traces_to: [FR-2, FR-3]
    depends_on: [GATE_0]
    outputs:
      - services/loyalty/printing/agent/windows-spooler.ts
      - services/loyalty/printing/agent/windows-spooler-simulated.ts
      - services/loyalty/printing/agent/windows-spooler-native.ts
      - services/loyalty/printing/agent/native/
      - __tests__/services/loyalty/printing/windows-spooler-escpos.test.ts
    gate: test-pass
    estimated_complexity: high

  WS_W3:
    name: Agent Windows-Service lifecycle (ADR-063 D1-D4)
    description: >
      CODEABLE: localhost request-authentication implementing the DEC-WIN-03 envelope (NOT inferred
      ad hoc inside loopback-agent.ts — audit P1-3) and a versioned caller<->agent protocol with
      incompatible-agent refusal, fail-closed (D4); loopback 127.0.0.1 bind only (D1). OPS/MANUAL
      (Gate W-B): install-as-Windows-Service using the SINGLE mechanism selected in DEC-WIN-02
      (audit P1-4 — "sc.exe/nssm" fork removed), auto-start at boot, supervised restart per approved
      recovery policy, named least-privilege operational owner — executed/verified on a real Windows
      host. request-auth lives in dedicated modules, not folded into the agent loop.
      AUTOMATION BACKBONE: the agent exposes machine-verifiable `/health` and `/diagnostics` endpoints
      (loopback only) that the governed provisioning + certification scripts (WS_W9 / WS_W6) probe —
      these are what let one command replace dozens of manual checks. Service lifecycle (install /
      uninstall / start / stop / recovery) is delivered as a CALLABLE PowerShell module
      (PrintAgentService.psm1), not a standalone manual script, so Provision-PrintAgent.ps1
      orchestrates it.
      DIAGNOSTICS DISCLOSURE BOUNDARY (audit P2-2 — invariant): /diagnostics binds loopback only,
      REQUIRES authentication, and returns ONLY {agent_version, protocol_version, service_health,
      configured_target_exists, native_module_loaded, spooler_connectivity_boolean}. It MUST NOT
      return credentials, secrets, authentication signatures, raw request bodies, full environment
      variables, or unrestricted filesystem paths. Native/Win32 detail stays in access-controlled
      local logs, never in the canonical /diagnostics response. agent-auth-version.test.ts asserts the
      allowed/forbidden field sets.
    executor: backend-service-builder
    executor_type: skill
    traces_to: [FR-7, FR-8, NFR-6]
    depends_on: [GATE_0, WS_W1, WS_W2]
    outputs:
      - services/loyalty/printing/agent/request-auth.ts
      - services/loyalty/printing/agent/request-auth-config.ts
      - services/loyalty/printing/agent/protocol-version.ts
      - services/loyalty/printing/agent/diagnostics-endpoint.ts
      - services/loyalty/printing/agent/loopback-agent.ts
      - scripts/print-agent/agent-service-entry.ts
      - scripts/print-agent/PrintAgentService.psm1
      - __tests__/services/loyalty/printing/agent-auth-version.test.ts
    gate: test-pass
    estimated_complexity: high

  WS_W5:
    name: Completion-observability evidence note (evidence-only, INV-6)
    description: >
      Bounded evidence note recording whether the Windows RAW path exposes GENUINE device-completion
      signals (not mere spooler acceptance). PRD-093 ships requested|submitted|failed|unknown with
      failure_domain=device FORBIDDEN regardless of the finding (INV-2/INV-6). Any positive evidence
      is filed as an ADR/FIB candidate for a SUCCESSOR slice — NOT admitted here. Default disposition:
      "no completion claim, deferred." Ships NO vocabulary, schema, DTO, copy, or ADR change. Gate is
      artifact-review (audit P2-2): the note must EXIST and contain the explicit disposition field.
    executor: lead-architect
    executor_type: skill
    traces_to: [FR-12]
    depends_on: [WS_W2]
    outputs:
      - docs/00-vision/epson/prd-093/WS-W5-COMPLETION-OBSERVABILITY-EVIDENCE.md
    gate: artifact-review
    estimated_complexity: low

  WS_W4:
    name: Update / signing / trust-boundary hardening (ADR-063 D4/D7/D1)
    description: >
      CODEABLE (per DEC-WIN-04): agent packaging script, a fail-closed manifest/package integrity
      verifier (NFR-7), a rollback script that disables the agent path with NO browser fallback
      (INV-5, D7), and FIXTURE tests that prove tampered-package and expired/invalid-signature
      rejection. Delivers the supply-chain acceptance TEMPLATE only. MANUAL/ENVIRONMENTAL (Gate W-B):
      the real code-signing certificate, the signed installer/binary, and populating the acceptance
      artifact — these stay PENDING until performed on the certification Windows host with the named
      signing authority (audit P2-3). Gate is script-test (audit P2-2): scripts pass dry-run/fixture
      tests including tamper + expiry rejection. PowerShell deliverables are validated by the
      script-test gate (audit P2-2): every generated .ps1 must pass PowerShell parser validation, and
      install/uninstall + rollback command construction is asserted by a dedicated test (dry-run /
      fixture where environment-safe). Real service installation remains manual; script syntax and
      deterministic command generation are pipeline-testable.
    executor: devops-pt2
    executor_type: skill
    traces_to: [FR-9, NFR-7]
    depends_on: [GATE_0, WS_W3]
    outputs:
      - scripts/print-agent/package-agent.ps1
      - scripts/print-agent/verify-integrity.ts
      - scripts/print-agent/rollback-agent.ps1
      - __tests__/scripts/print-agent/integrity-rejection.test.ts
      - __tests__/scripts/print-agent/ps-command-construction.test.ts
      - docs/00-vision/epson/prd-093/SUPPLY-CHAIN-ACCEPTANCE-TEMPLATE.md
    gate: script-test
    estimated_complexity: medium

  WS_W7:
    name: Security posture — Windows localhost (GATE-SEC-1 certification-grade)
    description: >
      IMPLEMENTER backend-service-builder; MANDATORY REVIEWER rls-expert (the registered security
      domain skill) — audit P2-4. The reviewer must sign off on the authentication envelope, replay
      resistance, credential storage, listener exposure, and the pre-spool rejection guarantee
      against the DEC-WIN-03 contract. CODEABLE: adversarial-request matrix test — missing auth,
      invalid auth, replayed authenticated request, expired timestamp/nonce, incompatible protocol
      version (fail-closed), malformed job_key, unknown printer_target_id — each REJECTED before any
      spooler submission and creating NO duplicate job. Confirms opaque printer_target_id resolved
      server-side (FR-10) and loopback-only bind. MANUAL (Gate W-B/W-C): Windows networking/firewall
      review that no LAN/public listener exists; localhost permission review.
    executor: backend-service-builder
    executor_type: skill
    traces_to: [FR-10, NFR-4]
    depends_on: [GATE_0, WS_W3]
    outputs:
      - __tests__/services/loyalty/printing/windows-localhost-security.test.ts
      - docs/00-vision/epson/prd-093/LOCALHOST-SECURITY-REVIEW.md
    gate: test-pass
    estimated_complexity: medium

  WS_W9:
    name: Windows provisioning automation (one governed command)
    description: >
      The single governed provisioning orchestrator `Provision-PrintAgent.ps1` that replaces dozens of
      manual setup steps. Parameters: -PrinterQueue -ServiceAccount -ConfigPath -PackagePath. It:
      validates prerequisites; verifies package signature (calls WS_W4 verify-integrity); installs
      files; applies ACLs; installs the Windows Service + startup/recovery (calls WS_W3
      PrintAgentService.psm1); generates the agent config file; binds loopback; validates the printer
      queue; starts the service; runs /health + /diagnostics + authentication success/failure probes;
      verifies NO LAN listener (firewall/listener inspection); and emits a machine-readable evidence
      report. Human still SUPPLIES the inputs (service account, signing cert, deployment owner,
      firewall/credential policy approval) — the script consumes them, it does not invent them. Gate
      script-test: PS parser validation + command-construction/dry-run assertions (real install manual).
    executor: devops-pt2
    executor_type: skill
    traces_to: [FR-7, FR-8, FR-10, NFR-4, NFR-6]
    depends_on: [WS_W3, WS_W4, WS_W7]
    outputs:
      - scripts/print-agent/Provision-PrintAgent.ps1
      - scripts/print-agent/PrintAgentEvidence.psm1
      - __tests__/scripts/print-agent/provision-command-construction.test.ts
      - docs/00-vision/epson/prd-093/PROVISIONING-EVIDENCE-REPORT-TEMPLATE.md
    gate: script-test
    estimated_complexity: high

  WS_WIN_SMOKE:
    name: Windows artifact loadability smoke (CONDITIONAL certification gate)
    description: >
      CONDITIONAL CERTIFICATION GATE (audit P1-1) — a trailing environment gate, NOT a build
      prerequisite. Runs the machine-verifiable subset of Provision-PrintAgent.ps1 (native deps
      install, module loads, service entry starts, /health + /diagnostics respond on 127.0.0.1,
      simulated non-print smoke) on a Windows runner and captures WINDOWS-ARTIFACT-SMOKE-RESULT.md.
      It BLOCKS only: windows_artifact_validated, gate_w_c_certified, production_rollout. It does NOT
      block: WS_W8, WS_W6 artifact generation, WS_DOD, code_complete, or artifacts_ready. This repo
      has NO Windows runner, so the gate is recorded MANUAL-PENDING (NOT pass) — the automated Linux
      pipeline still reaches code-complete + artifacts-ready; windows_artifact_validated stays false
      until a Windows runner (or the operator via WINDOWS-ARTIFACT-SMOKE-CHECKLIST.md) executes it.
    executor: qa-specialist
    executor_type: skill
    traces_to: [NFR-6, G5]
    depends_on: [WS_W9]
    outputs:
      - docs/00-vision/epson/prd-093/WINDOWS-ARTIFACT-SMOKE-RESULT.md
    gate: windows-artifact-smoke
    estimated_complexity: low

  WS_W8:
    name: Regression parity with the Linux exemplar
    description: >
      Adds windows_spooler to the ONE printer-contract-parity suite so windows_spooler + cups + fake
      return IDENTICAL canonical result/failure vocabulary (GATE-PLATFORM-2). Extends the boundary
      lint to ban Windows API imports/types, winspool function names, printer-queue impl identifiers,
      ESC/POS byte constants, and native-binding machinery ABOVE the adapter/agent boundary (NFR-5,
      GATE-PLATFORM-1). Asserts print_attempt audit contract-identical per the §5.4 definition
      (schema/vocabulary/behavior, NOT byte-identical). Re-asserts GATE-DOM-1 (no promo_coupon/ledger
      write) and GATE-DUP-1 (idempotent replay / reprint lineage / adapter-fires-once) on the Windows
      path. Boundary-lint convergence guard — depends on ALL implementation workstreams.
    executor: qa-specialist
    executor_type: skill
    traces_to: [FR-5, FR-6, FR-11, NFR-2, NFR-5]
    depends_on: [WS_W1, WS_W2, WS_W3, WS_W4, WS_W7]
    outputs:
      - __tests__/services/loyalty/printing/printer-contract-parity.test.ts
      - __tests__/services/loyalty/printing/windows-audit-parity.int.test.ts
      - eslint.config.mjs
    gate: test-pass
    estimated_complexity: medium

  WS_W6:
    name: Gate E2 certification automation (one governed command + harness)
    description: >
      Delivers the second governed command `Invoke-GateE2Certification.ps1`, which automates every
      machine-verifiable Gate E2 check against the provisioned agent (WS_W9) and pauses ONLY for
      physical confirmations. Automated: transport tests, authentication tests, replay/expired-request
      tests (calls WS_W7 check functions), reboot-recovery verification, package signature/integrity
      verification (calls WS_W4), log collection, and evidence-report generation. Human-confirmation
      prompts (Y/N only): "Did exactly one receipt print?", "Was the content legible?", "Did the
      cutter operate correctly?", "Was any duplicate paper emitted?". CODEABLE (CI): the Phase-1
      distinguishability matrix re-run over the simulated WindowsSpooler — first-print, printer-offline
      per the §5.5 custody matrix (pre-acceptance rejection -> failed; spooler accepts custody while
      offline -> submitted, never relabeled; post-submit ambiguity -> unknown), retry/idempotent
      replay (no duplicate), explicit reprint lineage, unknown-state duplicate-risk ack, layout/feed/
      cut. MANUAL (Gate W-C): real host + real TM-T88V; the script collects evidence and the final
      Gate E2 sign-off is recorded BEFORE rollout.
    executor: qa-specialist
    executor_type: skill
    traces_to: [G5, FR-6]
    depends_on: [WS_W8, WS_W5, WS_W9]
    outputs:
      - scripts/print-agent/Invoke-GateE2Certification.ps1
      - __tests__/services/loyalty/printing/windows-distinguishability.test.ts
      - __tests__/scripts/print-agent/certification-command-construction.test.ts
      - docs/00-vision/epson/prd-093/GATE-E2-CERTIFICATION-CHECKLIST.md
      - docs/00-vision/epson/prd-093/WINDOWS-ARTIFACT-SMOKE-CHECKLIST.md
    gate: test-pass
    estimated_complexity: high

  WS_DOD:
    name: Automated Definition of Done
    description: >
      Final automated DoD convergence (audit P2-1). Runs the full repo gate set so the build cannot
      declare code-complete without proving the application still type-checks, lints (boundary lint
      green), tests, and BUILDS. The DoD report MUST record per-status fields, not just pass/fail
      (audit P2-3): `powershell_parser_validated: true|false` and `powershell_parser_status:
      passed|pending` (pending when pwsh was unavailable on the Linux runner). CERTIFICATION RULE:
      `windows_artifact_validated` cannot become true while `powershell_parser_status` is `pending` —
      so a deferred parser check constrains certification status without blocking code-complete.
    executor: qa-specialist
    executor_type: skill
    traces_to: [DoD]
    depends_on: [WS_W6]
    outputs:
      - docs/00-vision/epson/prd-093/EXEC-093-DOD-REPORT.md
    gate: build
    estimated_complexity: low

# Execution Phases (topologically sorted)
execution_phases:
  - name: Phase 0 - Windows Platform Decision Gate
    parallel: [GATE_0]
    gates: [decision-approved]

  - name: Phase 1 - Gate W-A Foundation
    parallel: [WS_W1, WS_W2]
    gates: [type-check, test-pass]

  - name: Phase 2 - Gate W-B Agent Lifecycle + Evidence
    parallel: [WS_W3, WS_W5]
    gates: [test-pass, artifact-review]

  - name: Phase 3 - Gate W-B/W-C Hardening + Security
    parallel: [WS_W4, WS_W7]
    gates: [script-test, test-pass]

  - name: Phase 4 - Windows Provisioning Automation (Provision-PrintAgent.ps1)
    parallel: [WS_W9]
    gates: [script-test]

  - name: Phase 5 - Gate W-C Regression Parity (boundary-lint convergence)
    parallel: [WS_W8]
    gates: [test-pass, lint]

  - name: Phase 6 - Gate E2 Certification Automation (Invoke-GateE2Certification.ps1)
    parallel: [WS_W6]
    gates: [test-pass]

  - name: Phase 7 - Automated Definition of Done (code-complete + artifacts-ready)
    parallel: [WS_DOD]
    gates: [type-check, lint, test-pass, build]

  # Phase 8 is a TRAILING CONDITIONAL certification gate — it does NOT block Phases 5-7.
  # Without a Windows runner it records MANUAL-PENDING; code-complete/artifacts-ready are already met.
  - name: Phase 8 - Windows Artifact Smoke (conditional certification gate)
    parallel: [WS_WIN_SMOKE]
    gates: [windows-artifact-smoke]

# Validation Gates
gates:
  decision-approved:
    command: "human review of PRD-093-WINDOWS-CERTIFICATION-DECISION-LEDGER.md"
    success_criteria: "DEC-WIN-01..04 each resolved with rationale + dependencies; human-approved"
  type-check:
    command: npm run type-check
    success_criteria: "Exit code 0"
  lint:
    command: npm run lint
    success_criteria: "Exit code 0, boundary lint green (GATE-PLATFORM-1)"
  test-pass:
    command: npm run test
    success_criteria: "Exit code 0, all printing suites pass"
  artifact-review:
    command: "verify required document exists and contains mandated fields"
    success_criteria: "WS-W5 evidence note exists with an explicit completion disposition field"
  script-test:
    command: "npm run test -- __tests__/scripts/print-agent/ && (command -v pwsh >/dev/null && pwsh -NoProfile -Command \"Get-ChildItem scripts/print-agent/*.ps1 | ForEach-Object { [System.Management.Automation.Language.Parser]::ParseFile($_.FullName,[ref]$null,[ref]$null) | Out-Null }\" || echo 'pwsh unavailable: parser check deferred; command-construction asserted in ps-command-construction.test.ts')"
    success_criteria: "integrity-rejection TS tests pass (tamper + expiry); every .ps1 parses (where pwsh available); install/uninstall + rollback command-construction assertions pass"
  windows-artifact-smoke:
    command: "(Windows runner) native deps install; service entry starts; module loads; health responds; simulated non-print smoke completes"
    success_criteria: "PASS only on a Windows runner; on Linux pipeline record MANUAL-PENDING (NOT pass) — Windows-artifact-validated stays false until a runner/operator executes it"
  build:
    command: npm run build
    success_criteria: "Exit code 0"
---

# EXEC-093 — POS Loyalty Instrument Printing: Windows Production Certification

> **Revision 2** — patched per the EXEC-093 audit. Adds the EXEC Gate 0 decision ledger (P1-1),
> a concrete native WindowsSpooler deliverable (P1-2), an implementable localhost-auth contract
> (P1-3), a single selected service mechanism (P1-4), a GATE-HW-2 retrospective manual-evidence
> reconciliation (P1-5), a scheduled final build/DoD phase (P2-1), and deliverable-faithful
> per-workstream gates (P2-2..P2-5, P3-1). Rev 4 split the automation surface into two governed
> commands. Rev 5 models WS_WIN_SMOKE as a trailing conditional certification gate (P1-1) and adds the
> diagnostics disclosure boundary (P2-2) and PowerShell parser status reporting (P2-3).

## Overview

This EXEC-SPEC realizes PRD-093: certifying the controlled loyalty-instrument printing path —
already proven on Linux/CUPS in PRD-092 — for the **Windows** production host. This is a **platform
realization below the established adapter boundary**, not an architecture redesign. The
`LoyaltyInstrumentPrinter<ReceiptDocument>` contract, `ReceiptDocument`, renderer, `print_attempt`
lifecycle, idempotency, and reprint lineage are **inherited unchanged** (INV-1). The work adds a
`windows_spooler` adapter, a `WindowsSpooler` RAW-winspool seam **plus a concrete native module**,
agent-as-Windows-Service hardening, localhost trust-boundary hardening, a signed install/update path,
and the Gate E2 certification artifacts.

This is **not "primarily wiring."** It introduces a new platform transport with real correctness
risk (the RAW-datatype mandate exists specifically to defeat the blank-output symptom), a new
localhost security surface (caller auth + version handshake + adversarial matrix), and a supply-chain
posture (signed binary, integrity-verified updates, rollback). The framing matches FIB-H §Q: prove
RAW transport first, harden as a managed service second, certify on real hardware third.

## EXEC Gate 0 — Windows Platform Decision Gate (audit P1-1)

PRD-093 §7.3 delegated four implementation decisions to EXEC. They MUST be resolved and human-approved
in `PRD-093-WINDOWS-CERTIFICATION-DECISION-LEDGER.md` **before any implementation workstream runs** —
otherwise executors independently invent incompatible bindings, auth schemes, hosting mechanisms, and
packaging models.

| ID | Decision | Must select / define |
|----|----------|----------------------|
| DEC-WIN-01 | RAW Winspool binding + native dependency | The concrete binding (e.g. N-API addon / FFI / bundled helper exe), its build toolchain, and runtime dependency surface |
| DEC-WIN-02 | Windows Service hosting + identity | **ONE** mechanism (sc.exe service-exe/wrapper **xor** NSSM **xor** other); third-party dependency status; startup/recovery config; log destination; install/uninstall idempotency; least-privilege identity; queue permissions |
| DEC-WIN-03 | Localhost authentication | sanctioned caller identity; credential provisioning + storage location; signed request fields; nonce/request-id uniqueness scope; timestamp tolerance; replay-cache lifetime + bound; rotation/revocation; protocol-version binding; pre-spool rejection mapping; **browser-bundle exclusion** |
| DEC-WIN-04 | Packaging / signing / update / rollback | packaging format; integrity-verification method (fail-closed); update model (no silent self-update / side-loading); rollback that disables the agent path with no browser fallback |

**Gate-0 invariant:** may instantiate the PRD contract but may **not** alter lifecycle vocabulary,
audit schema, printer family, browser fallback, or network scope (FIB-H §L). Decisions feed WS_W2
(DEC-WIN-01), WS_W3 (DEC-WIN-02/03), WS_W4 (DEC-WIN-04), WS_W7 (DEC-WIN-03 review).

## Native implementation is a build deliverable, not a certification chore (audit P1-2)

WS_W2 ships three artifacts: the seam (`windows-spooler.ts`), a deterministic simulator
(`windows-spooler-simulated.ts`) for CI, and a **concrete native module**
(`windows-spooler-native.ts`) implementing DEC-WIN-01. **Gate W-A is manual *execution* of the
delivered native module on Windows — not manual *creation* of it.** Native acceptance: opens the
configured printer; `StartDocPrinter pDatatype="RAW"`; `StartPagePrinter` where the binding requires;
`WritePrinter` writes the complete buffer (partial writes rejected or completed deterministically);
guaranteed `EndPagePrinter`/`EndDocPrinter`/`ClosePrinter` cleanup; Win32/native diagnostics stay
agent-local. The simulator and seam enable certification; they do **not** substitute for the module.

## GATE-HW-2 precondition — retrospective manual-evidence reconciliation (audit P1-1)

The frontmatter `gate_hw_2_precondition` records the Linux Phase-1 gate as **closed by retrospective
manual-evidence reconciliation**: the PRD-092 Linux/CUPS real-device acceptance **was performed
manually**; the gate stayed open only because its governance state was not reconciled (the PRD-092
Implementation Precis carried a stale `OPEN` status — that stale record *is* the bookkeeping being
corrected). The reconciliation artifact is
`docs/00-vision/epson/prd-093/GATE-HW-2-RECONCILIATION.md`; it records the observed manual evidence
after the fact and the test is **not** repeated. This establishes the known-good Phase-1
physical-printer baseline and **does not** claim Windows certification — Windows Gate E2 (Gate W-C)
remains independently mandatory.

## Codeable vs. Manual tiering (PRD-092 GATE-HW-2 precedent)

This build runs in a **Linux repository**. It delivers every **codeable** artifact and verifies it
with type-check / lint / test / build. The following acceptance items are inherently **off-platform /
hardware** and are recorded as **MANUAL certification gates** — the build reaches *code-complete +
artifacts-ready*, not *certified*. `WINDOWS_CERTIFICATION_REQUIRED` (ADR-062 D8) stays in place until
a human records the manual evidence.

| Manual gate | Enabling artifact delivered by this build | Recorded in |
|-------------|-------------------------------------------|-------------|
| Gate W-A — real-device RAW print on TM-T88V | concrete native module + seam + simulator + ESC/POS bytes (WS_W2) | GATE-E2-CERTIFICATION-CHECKLIST.md |
| Gate W-B — reboot/recovery + real signed cert | install/recovery scripts + packaging/integrity verifier + fixture tests (WS_W3/WS_W4) | SUPPLY-CHAIN-ACCEPTANCE.md (PENDING until populated on cert host) |
| Gate W-B/W-C — Windows firewall/LAN-listener review | localhost security test matrix + review doc (WS_W7) | LOCALHOST-SECURITY-REVIEW.md |
| Windows-artifact loadability smoke | checklist authored by WS_W6; execution result captured by WS_WIN_SMOKE | CHECKLIST.md (WS_W6) + WINDOWS-ARTIFACT-SMOKE-RESULT.md (WS_WIN_SMOKE) |
| Gate W-C — Gate E2 sign-off | distinguishability harness + checklist (WS_W6) | GATE-E2-CERTIFICATION-CHECKLIST.md |

> **P2-5 note:** real printer output stays manual, but Windows-artifact *loadability* (native deps
> install, service starts, module loads, health responds, simulated smoke completes) is automatable on
> a Windows runner via the `windows-artifact-smoke` gate and should not be deferred to the hardware
> session. This repo has no Windows runner, so that gate is **conditional** — its checklist is
> delivered; its execution is gated on runner availability.

## Dependency graph & execution order

```
Phase 0 (decision): GATE_0 ── DEC-WIN-01..04 (human-approved ledger)
                       │
Phase 1 (W-A):  WS_W1 ── windows adapter        WS_W2 ── RAW seam + simulator + NATIVE module
                       └──────────────┬──────────────────┘
Phase 2 (W-B):  WS_W3 ── agent lifecycle/auth     WS_W5 ── completion evidence (← WS_W2)
                       ├───────────────┐
Phase 3 (W-B/C): WS_W4 ── sign/pkg+fixtures  WS_W7 ── localhost security (rls-expert review)
                       └───────┬────────┘
Phase 4 (prov):  WS_W9 ── Provision-PrintAgent.ps1 (one governed command; ← W3,W4,W7)
Phase 5 (W-C):  WS_W8 ── regression parity + boundary-lint convergence (← W1,W2,W3,W4,W7)
Phase 6 (E2):   WS_W6 ── Invoke-GateE2Certification.ps1 + harness (← W8, W5, W9)
Phase 7 (DoD):  WS_DOD ── type-check + lint + test + BUILD   → CODE-COMPLETE + ARTIFACTS-READY
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Phase 8 (cond): WS_WIN_SMOKE ── conditional cert gate (← W9; MANUAL-PENDING w/o runner)
                 does NOT block Phases 5-7; gates only windows_artifact_validated / Gate W-C / rollout
```

## Completion states (audit P1-1)

These are distinct and must not be conflated. The Linux pipeline can reach the first two with **no**
Windows runner; the last two require the real host.

| State | Reached when | Requires Windows? |
|-------|--------------|-------------------|
| `code_complete` | automated Linux gates pass (type-check/lint/test/**build** — WS_DOD) | no |
| `artifacts_ready` | provisioning + certification tooling produced (WS_W9, WS_W6) | no |
| `windows_artifact_validated` | WS_WIN_SMOKE passes on a Windows runner (also requires PowerShell parser validation PASSED, not pending — P2-3) | yes |
| `production_certified` | Gate E2 passes + human sign-off recorded | yes (real host + device) |

## Automation boundary — two governed commands (operator-facing)

The build delivers the scriptable surface as **two governed PowerShell commands** backed by the
agent's own loopback `/health` + `/diagnostics` endpoints, so the operator runs *two commands*, not
dozens of steps.

```
.\Provision-PrintAgent.ps1 -PrinterQueue "EPSON TM-T88V" -ServiceAccount ".\d3lt-print-agent" `
                           -ConfigPath "C:\ProgramData\d3lt\print-agent" -PackagePath ".\d3lt-print-agent.zip"
.\Invoke-GateE2Certification.ps1
```

| Scriptable & automated (delivered) | Human / organizational input only |
|------------------------------------|-----------------------------------|
| Printer-queue discovery + validation | Selecting the actual service account |
| Service install / uninstall / start / stop / recovery policy | Supplying the signing certificate |
| Service-account permission checks | Naming the deployment owner |
| Config-file generation | Approving firewall + credential policy |
| Loopback bind verification | Installing / physically connecting the printer |
| Firewall rule inspection (no LAN listener) | Visually confirming layout / quality / feed / cut |
| Native module load test; `/health` probe | Confirming whether a physical duplicate was emitted |
| Auth success/failure, replay, expired-request tests | Final production certification sign-off |
| Package signature + installer integrity verification | |
| Upgrade + rollback procedure | |
| Reboot-survival verification; log collection | |
| Gate E2 evidence collection → report | |

`Provision-PrintAgent.ps1` (WS_W9) validates prereqs → verifies package signature → installs files →
applies ACLs → installs the service → configures startup/recovery → binds loopback → validates the
queue → starts the service → runs health/auth checks → verifies no LAN listener → emits an evidence
report. `Invoke-GateE2Certification.ps1` (WS_W6) automates the machine-verifiable transport/auth/
replay/reboot/package checks and pauses only for physical Y/N confirmations
(`Did exactly one receipt print? [Y/N]`, `Legible? [Y/N]`, `Cutter correct? [Y/N]`,
`Any duplicate paper? [Y/N]`), then writes the Gate E2 evidence report.

## Hard invariants binding every workstream (PRD §1a)

- **INV-1** inherited contract / renderer unchanged — no renderer redesign.
- **INV-2** frozen vocabulary `requested|submitted|failed|unknown`; `printed`/`acknowledged`/
  `completed`/`PrinterFault`/`failure_domain=device` **forbidden** in DTOs, UI, persisted schema —
  no in-PRD reopening path.
- **INV-3** `testPrint()` is adapter-direct and writes **zero** `print_attempt` rows.
- **INV-4** audit **contract-identical** (per §5.4 — schema/vocabulary/behavior, NOT byte-identical).
- **INV-5** no automatic `window.print()` browser fallback on any path.
- **INV-6** completion is evidence-only (WS_W5).
- **GATE-PLATFORM-1 (NFR-1/NFR-5)** — no Windows/winspool/ESC-POS implementation types or strings
  above the adapter/agent boundary; device machinery confined to a **single platform-confined native
  implementation module** under `agent/` (audit P3-1).

## Anti-fork directive (WS_W1)

The `windows_spooler` adapter MUST import and reuse `mapAgentResponseToOutcome`,
`mapTransportErrorToOutcome`, and `deriveJobKey` from `cups-adapter.ts` (or a shared module hoisted
from it). A copied/forked mapper is the most likely way the canonical vocabulary silently drifts and
defeats GATE-PLATFORM-2. The parity suite (WS_W8) is the guard that catches drift.

## Governance / ownership

- **Bounded context:** single context `LoyaltyService.InstrumentPrinting` (`services/loyalty/printing/`).
  **No SRM amendment** — `print_attempt` ownership inherited, no schema migration (INV-4).
- **No SRL admission** — §7a `srl_required: false`; INV-2 forbids new canonical terms.
- **No new public API surface** — `renders_derived_value_surface: false`; Gate B classifies `none`.
- **Security review (audit P2-4):** WS_W7 is implemented by backend-service-builder with a **mandatory
  rls-expert review** (registered security domain skill) of the auth envelope, replay resistance,
  credential storage, listener exposure, and pre-spool rejection guarantee.

## Phase-3 file-contention note

WS_W4 (devops packaging → `scripts/print-agent/` + fixtures + docs) and WS_W7 (security → `__tests__/`
+ agent + docs) have **disjoint** output file sets, so parallel dispatch is safe. If an executor's
refined output drifts onto `loopback-agent.ts` in both, fall back to sequential or `isolation: worktree`.
